name: backend-dev-guidelines
description: Comprehensive backend development guide for Supabase Edge Functions + PostgreSQL. Use when working with Supabase (database, auth, storage, realtime), Edge Functions, PostgreSQL, Row-Level Security (RLS), Resend email, Stripe payments, or TypeScript backend patterns. Covers database design, auth flows, Edge Function patterns, RLS policies, email integration, payment processing, and deployment to Supabase.
capabilities: Provides Supabase Edge Function patterns with Deno runtime
Shows PostgreSQL schema design with Row-Level Security policies
Demonstrates JWT authentication and authorization patterns
Covers Zod validation for type-safe input handling
Includes Resend email and Stripe payment integration examples
Offers controller, service, and repository layer patterns
author: DojoCodingLabs
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance files. Static scanner flagged 755 issues but all are false positives caused by pattern-matching on code examples within documentation. Previous Claude audit confirmed no security concerns. All code examples demonstrate legitimate security best practices for Supabase backends.
valueStatement: Writing backend code for Supabase can be complex with many moving parts. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for Edge Functions, database design, Row-Level Security, and integrations so you can ship faster with fewer bugs.
userTitle: Build Supabase backends with Edge Functions
limitations: Does not provide executable code or scripts to run
Requires Supabase account and CLI for local development
Skill is a guide, not a replacement for understanding underlying technologies
Examples are scaffold templates that need customization for production use

useCases.title: Build serverless APIs
useCases.description: Create Supabase Edge Functions for your web or mobile application with proper auth and validation.

useCases.title: Design secure databases
useCases.description: Design PostgreSQL schemas with Row-Level Security to enforce data access policies at the database level.

useCases.title: Integrate payments and email
useCases.description: Add Stripe subscription handling and Resend transactional emails to your application.

promptTemplates.title: New Edge Function
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Supabase Edge Function called create-user that validates input with Zod, verifies the JWT token, and inserts a record into the users table with RLS policies.

promptTemplates.title: Database Schema
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a PostgreSQL schema for a posts table with user ownership, published status, and proper indexes. Include RLS policies for read and write access.

promptTemplates.title: Auth Middleware
promptTemplates.prompt: Add JWT verification to an existing Edge Function and implement role-based access control using custom claims in the user metadata.

promptTemplates.title: Stripe Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler Edge Function that verifies the signature, handles checkout.session.completed events, and updates user subscription status in the database.

bestPractices: Always verify JWT tokens at the start of every Edge Function to prevent unauthorized access
Enable Row-Level Security on ALL tables containing user data for database-level protection
Validate all input with Zod schemas before any database operations or business logic
Use environment variables for all secrets and API keys - never hardcode credentials in code
antiPatterns: Skipping JWT verification in production Edge Functions exposes your API to unauthorized access
Disabling RLS to simplify development defeats the purpose of Supabase security features
Using console.error for error handling instead of proper HTTP responses breaks client integration
Bypassing the Supabase client to query the database directly circumvents RLS protections

faq.question: What Supabase features does this skill cover?
faq.answer: Edge Functions, PostgreSQL database, Row-Level Security, Auth, Storage, and Realtime subscriptions.

faq.question: Can I use these patterns with regular Express servers?
faq.answer: Yes. Many patterns (validation, services, repositories) work with any Node.js backend framework.

faq.question: Does this skill require Supabase CLI?
faq.answer: Supabase CLI is recommended for local development and testing Edge Functions before deployment.

faq.question: Is my data safe with the examples shown?
faq.answer: The examples emphasize security best practices including RLS, JWT verification, and environment variable secrets.

faq.question: Why use Edge Functions instead of a traditional server?
faq.answer: Edge Functions scale automatically, run globally at edge locations, and integrate tightly with Supabase services.

faq.question: How does this compare to using the Supabase JavaScript client?
faq.answer: Edge Functions run server-side for operations that require elevated permissions or secret API keys.
